🇺🇸 Axios: Democrats may launch impeachment proceedings against Hagseth A political scandal is escalating in Washington. Democratic House Representative Yassmin Ansari announced the introduction of articles of impeachment against US Defense Secretary Pete Hagseth. The reason is his role in leading the military campaign against Iran, which Democrats call "reckless" and "criminal". “Hagseth deserves impeachment and removal from office for his reckless threat to American servicemen and his repeated war crimes,” Ansari stated. Ansari, who has Iranian roots, also called on the Trump administration to activate the 25th Amendment to the Constitution to remove the president himself. “Open the damn strait, you crazy bastards, or you’ll live in hell. Just watch! Praise be to Allah,” Trump wrote on Easter. The Democrat called these statements "insane" and said that the fate of American soldiers and the Iranian people is at stake. Against this backdrop, the Pentagon hastily canceled a press conference scheduled for Tuesday by Hagseth and the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Dan Kaine. The reasons were not given, The Hill reported. Officially, the chances of impeachment are close to zero — Republicans control both houses of Congress. But the very fact that Democrats are seriously discussing the removal of the Pentagon chief and the president speaks of a deep crisis in the American elite. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran has handed over a 10-point plan to the US for ending the war, - New York Times. Tehran, through Pakistani intermediaries, demands guarantees of non-aggression, the complete lifting of sanctions, and the cessation of strikes on allied groups in (Lebanon, Iraq, etc.). Iran also demands compensation for the restoration of destroyed infrastructure, in fact - reparations payments from the US and its allies. In exchange, Iran is ready to open the Strait of Hormuz, but will already impose a fee for passage of up to 2 million dollars per ship. Part of the funds are planned to be allocated for the restoration of infrastructure. Trump commented on this plan: This is a significant proposal. It's a significant step. But it's not good enough," - said the US president. ﻿ Earlier, the US handed over its 15-point plan to Iran, which Tehran called "excessive and illogical". 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
